subject: Physics
title: Earth's Magnetic Field Flips
description: A 60-second lesson on Earth's Magnetic Field Flips.

hook.text: Earth's magnetic poles have swapped places hundreds of times. The next flip could happen within thousands of years.

buildup.text: Earth's field is generated by convection currents in its molten iron outer core—a natural dynamo.

discovery.text: Rocks record the magnetic direction when they form. Studying ancient rocks revealed a history of reversals.

twist.text: The last reversal was 780,000 years ago. During a flip, the field weakens and multiple poles can appear.

climax.text: A weakened field during a flip could increase radiation reaching Earth's surface and disrupt navigation systems.

punchline.text: North becomes south. It's happened before. It will happen again.

quiz.question: What generates Earth's magnetic field?
options: Convection currents in the molten iron outer core
Permanent magnets at the Earth's center
The rotation of the Earth's crust
